title: Chapter XIV. 29–31.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Our Lord and Saviour, Jesus Christ, had said unto His disciples, “If ye loved me, ye would surely rejoice, because I go unto the Father; for the Father is greater than I.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And that He so spake in His servant-form, and not in that of God, wherein He is equal with the Father, is well known to faith as it resides in the minds of the pious, not as it is feigned by the scornful and senseless.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And then He added, “And now I have told you before it come to pass, that, when it is come to pass, ye might believe.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What can He mean by this, when the fact rather is, that a man ought, before it comes to pass, to believe that which demands his belief?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it forms the very encomium of faith when that which is believed is not seen.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what greatness is there in believing what is seen, as in those words of the same Lord, when, in reproving a disciple, He said, “Because thou hast seen, thou hast believed; blessed are they that see not, and yet believe.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And I hardly know whether any one can be said to believe what he sees; for this same faith is thus defined in the epistle addressed to the Hebrews: “Now faith is the substance of those that hope, the assurance of things not seen.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, if faith is in things that are believed, and that, too, in things which are not seen, what mean these words of the Lord, “And now I have told you before it come to pass, that, when it is come to pass, ye might believe”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ought He not rather to have said, And now I have told you before it come to pass, that ye may believe what, when it is come to pass, ye shall see?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For even he who was told, “Because thou hast seen, thou hast believed,” did not believe only what he saw; but he saw one thing, and believed another: for he saw Him as man, and believed Him to be God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He perceived and touched the living flesh, which he had seen in the act of dying, and he believed in the Deity infolded in that flesh.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so he believed with the mind what he did not see, by the help of that which was apparent to his bodily senses.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But though we may be said to believe what we see, just as every one says that he believes his own eyes, yet that is not to be mistaken for the faith which is built up by God in our souls; but from things that are seen, we are brought to believe in those which are invisible.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore, beloved, in the passage before us, when our Lord says, “And now I have told you before it come to pass, that, when it is come to pass, ye might believe;” by the words, “when it is come to pass,” He certainly means, that they would yet see Him after His death, alive, and ascending to His Father; at the sight of which they should then be compelled to believe that He was indeed the Christ, the Son of the living God, seeing He could do such a thing, even after predicting it, and also could predict it before He did it: and this they should then believe, not with a new, but with an augmented faith; or at least [with a faith] that had been impaired by His death, and was now repaired by His resurrection.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it was not that they had not previously also believed Him to be the Son of God, but when His own predictions were actually fulfilled in Him, that faith, which was still weak at the time of His here speaking to them, and at the time of His death almost ceased to exist, sprang up again into new life and increased vigor.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what says He next?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Hereafter I will not talk much with you; for the prince of this world cometh;” and who is that, but the devil?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And hath nothing in me;” that is to say, no sin at all.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For by such words He points to the devil, as the prince, not of His creatures, but of sinners, whom He here designates by the name of this world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And as often as the name of the world is used in a bad sense, He is pointing only to the lovers of such a world; of whom it is elsewhere recorded, “Whosoever will be a friend of this world, becomes the enemy of God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Far be it from us, then, so to understand the devil as prince of the world, as if he wielded the government of the whole world, that is, of heaven and earth, and all that is in them; of which sort of world it was said, when we were lecturing on Christ the Word, “And the world was made by Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The whole world therefore, from the highest heavens to the lowest earth, is subject to the Creator, not to the deserter; to the Redeemer, not to the destroyer; to the Deliverer, not to the enslaver; to the Teacher, not to the deceiver.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And in what sense the devil is to be understood as the prince of the world, is still more clearly unfolded by the Apostle Paul, who, after saying, “We wrestle not against flesh and blood,” that is, against men, went on to say, “but against principalities and powers, and the world-rulers of this darkness.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For in the very next word he has explained what he meant by “world,” when he added, “of this darkness;” so that no one, by the name of the world, should understand the whole creation, of which in no sense are fallen angels the rulers.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Of this darkness,” he says, that is, of the lovers of this world: of whom, nevertheless, there were some elected, not from any deserving of their own, but by the grace of God, to whom he says, “Ye were sometimes darkness; but now are ye light in the Lord.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For all have been under the rulers of this darkness, that is, [under the rulers] of wicked men, or darkness, as it were, in subjection to darkness: but “thanks be to God, who hath delivered us,” says the same apostle, “from the power of darkness, and hath translated us into the kingdom of the Son of His love.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And in Him the prince of this world, that is, of this darkness, had nothing; for neither did He come with sin as God, nor had His flesh any hereditary taint of sin in its procreation by the Virgin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And, as if it were said to Him, Why, then, dost Thou die, if Thou hast no sin to merit the punishment of death?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He immediately added, “But that the world may know that I love the Father, and as the Father gave me commandment, even so I do: arise, let us go hence.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He was sitting at table with those who were similarly occupied.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But “let us go,” He said, and whither, but to the place where He, who had nothing in Him deserving of death, was to be delivered up to death?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But He had the Father’s commandment to die, as the very One of whom it had been foretold, “Then I paid for that which I took not away;” and so appointed to pay death to the full, while owing it nothing, and to redeem us from the death that was our due.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For Adam had seized on sin as a prey, when, deceived, he presumptuously stretched forth his hand to the tree, and attempted to invade the incommunicable name of that Godhead which was disallowed him, and with which the Son of God was endowed by nature, and not by robbery.
